metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the ellipsis in AC3's proving command with a complete, runnable command, e.g. `python -c "import yaml, sys; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in sys.argv[1:]]" .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml`.
findings.finding: AC3's YAML-validation proving command is specified as `python -c "import yaml; ..."` with an ellipsis, leaving the actual parse-and-validate logic unspecified; an executor cannot run this command as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'YAML remains valid (`python -c "import yaml; ..."`)...'
findings.suggested_fix: python -c "import yaml, sys; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in sys.argv[1:]]" .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the existing `actions/cache@v4` pip-cache block in `eval.yml`'s `eval-live` job (lines 91-96) with the new `cache: 'pip'` input on its `setup-python` step — the plan should specify whether the manual cache block is removed (superseded) or kept, and update the AC/scope to reflect this.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'No CI workflow enables pip dependency caching' — this premise is directly refuted by eval.yml lines 91-96, which already implements a manual pip cache via actions/cache@v4 (path: ~/.cache/pip, key: pip-eval-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('pyproject.toml') }}) on the eval-live job. The plan's implementation will add cache: 'pip' to that same setup-python step alongside the existing manual cache block, potentially creating two active pip-cache mechanisms for that job, or the author may intend to remove the manual block — but neither action nor the interaction is addressed by the plan.
findings.location: Plan Summary section ('No CI workflow enables pip dependency caching') vs .github/workflows/eval.yml lines 88-96
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope or What section, add a note: 'eval.yml eval-live already has a manual actions/cache@v4 pip block; replace it with the setup-python cache: pip input to avoid duplicate cache mechanisms.' Then update AC1's grep verification to also confirm the manual actions/cache@v4 pip block is removed from that job.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete, anchored grep (or yq) command to AC1 that asserts every `uses: actions/setup-python` block in `.github/workflows/` contains both `cache: 'pip'` and `cache-dependency-path:`, e.g. using `yq` or a two-pass grep with word-boundary anchoring.
findings.finding: AC1's grep-based proof is gestured at ('verifiable by grep') but no concrete grep command is provided; without an anchored pattern the executor may write a grep that produces false positives (e.g. matching `cache:` inside a comment) or silently misses steps in files whose extension differs.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: '...verifiable by grep: no dep-installing setup-python step lacks the two cache* inputs.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan whether the two `@v5` steps in `reconcile-bridge.yml` and `reconcile-bridge-canary.yml` should be left at `@v5` (confirming `cache:` works there) or bumped to `@v6` as part of this change — document the decision explicitly in the Scope section.
findings.finding: Two workflows in scope (reconcile-bridge.yml and reconcile-bridge-canary.yml) pin `actions/setup-python@v5` while the rest use `@v6`; the plan's scope section does not acknowledge this version discrepancy. Although `cache: 'pip'` does exist in v5, the plan's implementation instructions do not call out that these two files use a different major version, leaving an implementer to discover the inconsistency at edit time. If an implementer upgrades those two to `@v6` as part of this PR (a natural impulse when touching them), that becomes an undocumented scope change. If they leave them at `@v5`, the plan gives no guidance.
findings.location: Scope section — reconcile-bridge.yml, reconcile-bridge-canary.yml; plan text 'Add `cache: 'pip'` + `cache-dependency-path: pyproject.toml` to EVERY `actions/setup-python` step'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in the Scope section: 'reconcile-bridge.yml and reconcile-bridge-canary.yml currently pin @v5; the `cache` input is supported from v2.1.0+ so @v5 is sufficient — do NOT bump the version as part of this change.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the interaction with the pre-existing `actions/cache@v4` pip-cache step in `eval.yml`'s `eval-live` job: either (a) remove the manual `actions/cache@v4` step and replace it with the `cache: 'pip'` on `setup-python` (consistent with the rest of the plan), or (b) explicitly exclude this already-cached step from AC-1 and document why — the plan currently neither acknowledges this step nor specifies which approach wins.
findings.finding: The `eval-live` job in `eval.yml` already has a manual `actions/cache@v4` step caching `~/.cache/pip` (lines 91-96). The plan's AC requires adding `cache: 'pip'` to EVERY dep-installing `setup-python` step without excepting this job. Doing so creates two simultaneous pip-cache mechanisms on the same directory in the same job — one triggered by `setup-python` (restore at step start, save at job end) and one by the explicit `actions/cache@v4` step — with different but overlapping cache keys. This leads to redundant restores, redundant saves, and potential cache-write contention where the manual step saves a stale post-install state that then overwrites (or races with) the setup-python post-install save.
findings.location: .github/workflows/eval.yml lines 88-100 (eval-live job); plan AC-1 ('EVERY `actions/setup-python` step that installs project deps')
findings.suggested_fix: In eval.yml, remove the explicit `actions/cache@v4` block (lines 91-96) from `eval-live` and add `cache: 'pip'` + `cache-dependency-path: pyproject.toml` to its `setup-python` step instead, making it consistent with all other jobs. Update AC-1 to add: 'where a pre-existing manual pip cache step exists, replace it with the setup-python built-in rather than adding both'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly address release.yml in the Scope section: either add it (if build/twine installs should be cached) or add a note explaining why it is intentionally excluded (e.g., 'build tooling only, not keyed on pyproject.toml extras').
findings.finding: release.yml has an actions/setup-python@v6 step that is followed by pip installs (build, twine) but is entirely absent from the plan's Scope list. The plan's AC says 'EVERY actions/setup-python step that installs project deps across all .github/workflows/*' — whether build/twine count as 'project deps' is ambiguous and the omission is unaddressed.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief note under Scope: 'release.yml is excluded — its pip installs (build, twine) are build tooling, not pyproject.toml extras; caching them keyed on pyproject.toml would provide no benefit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Note in the plan that reconcile-bridge.yml and reconcile-bridge-canary.yml use actions/setup-python@v5 (not @v6), confirm cache: 'pip' is supported on v5, and clarify whether the version will be left as-is or normalized.
findings.finding: reconcile-bridge.yml and reconcile-bridge-canary.yml use actions/setup-python@v5 (not @v6 as used elsewhere). The plan does not acknowledge this version difference, and the AC states the YAML is 'otherwise byte-for-byte unchanged apart from the two added cache* inputs' — an auditor checking the diff will see @v5 and may be confused about whether that is intentional.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical in the Scope list entry for these two files: '(currently @v5; cache: pip is supported on v4+, so the version will not be bumped — two cache* inputs added only).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Narrow the leaf's Scope to match the parent's declared boundary (gerrit-verify.yaml, test.yml, and _optionality.yml as a direct dependency of gerrit-verify), OR update the parent epic's Scope IN clause to explicitly authorize caching across all CI workflows before re-reviewing this leaf.
findings.finding: The leaf's scope extends well beyond the parent epic's declared boundary. The parent (ce0e-f618-a2a4-4ee4) scopes pip caching to 'gerrit-verify.yaml (and test.yml for parity)' in its Scope IN clause. The leaf instead targets ALL .github/workflows/* — including eval.yml, eval-plan-review-container.yml, external-integration.yml, reconcile-bridge.yml, reconcile-bridge-canary.yml, mirror-guard.yml, verify-identity.yaml, and _optionality.yml — none of which are named or implied by the parent's scope.
findings.location: Leaf Scope (files) section; Parent epic Scope IN clause: 'Applied to `gerrit-verify.yaml` (and `test.yml` for parity)'
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) trim the leaf's Scope (files) list to gerrit-verify.yaml, _optionality.yml (reusable called by gerrit-verify), and test.yml — exactly what the parent authorizes — and open a separate follow-up task/epic for the remaining 7 workflows; OR (b) update the parent epic's Scope IN clause to read 'pip caching on all `setup-python` steps across all .github/workflows/*' and re-earn plan-review attestation on the parent before re-claiming this leaf.

findings.finding: The session log explicitly names pytest-xdist (parallel test execution) as a requirement for speeding up gerrit-verify CI, but the plan contains no mention of xdist, no stated rationale for deferring it, and the ticket graph shows only a 'relates_to' link (not a child) to 4307-c757-6e30-4f6e — leaving it unverifiable whether xdist is covered elsewhere.

The session log explicitly names per-test performance logging as a requirement, but the plan contains no mention of it and provides no rationale for its exclusion; it is not resolvable to any child ticket in the provided graph.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
